weather_report:
West Perth leans into late morning under a pale, generous blue, with only a few light clouds idling across the sun. The light feels clean and honest, edges sharp, colors unmuted. Expect the sunshine to stay in charge through early afternoon with just a scatter of fair-weather cloud; even in late April the midday UV can sneak up on you, likely moderate to brisk for a few hours, so a hat, sunglasses, and a dab of sunscreen are sensible. The day reads as open and agreeable, no drama at the horizon.

Dress for ease: breathable layers you can shrug off as the sun warms, comfortable shoes, and something with a brim. These are good hours for quiet, ordinary errands done on foot, picking up bread or a library hold, watering the balcony pots, or claiming a shaded bench with a paperback and a thermos. If lunch outdoors calls, the sky looks set to oblige with bright company and little fuss.
